title: Proof Boundary — Security Automation Needs Guardrails
supported_claims: Security automation should operate within explicit permissions, scope limits, monitoring, incident-response, and recovery controls.
Secure software practices should be integrated throughout the development lifecycle rather than added only after deployment.
Authority and review requirements should be proportionate to the consequence of an automated action.
unsupported_claims: Any product-specific capability or security claim not revalidated against current documentation.
Any universal claim that automation is unsafe or that guardrails eliminate operational risk.
Any quantified claim about incident-response improvement or risk reduction without direct evidence.
